name: Breaded Tofu Cutlets
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Firm or extra-firm tofu, frozen (see note)
1/2 cup Whole-wheat pastry flour or white flour
recipeInstructions:
1. To defrost tofu, place a plastic bag filled with frozen tofu into a bowl of hot water. After about 15 minutes, drain the bowl and add more hot water. Repeat until tofu is soft. Remove tofu from the bag, rinse, and squeeze out excess water between two plates while pressing.
2. Cut tofu horizontally into 1/2 inch thick slices.
3. In a large shallow pot, combine water, ginger, and soy sauce. Simmer sliced tofu in the marinade for 20 minutes. Using a spatula, remove tofu and gently press to squeeze out excess liquid between fingertips. Set aside.
4. To make dip, combine soft tofu, 1/2 cup water, and 2 tbsp soy sauce in a blender or food processor; pour onto a large flat plate. Place whole-wheat flour on a second flat plate and cornmeal on a third.
5. Dredge each slice of marinated tofu first in flour, then in batter, and last in cornmeal.
6. Heat oil to sizzling in a large skillet. Deep or shallow fry each cutlet until both sides are golden brown. Drain on paper towels.
7. Serve with a dollop of tartar sauce and sprig of fresh parsley. Note: To freeze tofu, place in a plastic bag, seal, and freeze for at least 48 hours before thawing. The color will be light amber, and when cooked, the texture will be pleasantly chewy. Makes 6 servings.
